claim: Event sourcing agent harnesses make debugging trivial because every side effect is logged as an event in an immutable append-only log
actionable: Design agent systems with event sourcing so that all state transitions are queryable: streaming chunks, tool calls, errors, and circuit breaker triggers all appear as events
source_title: Make your own event-sourced agent harness using stream processors

claim: Separate reduce (state derivation) from after_append (side effects) so agents can catch up on missed events without replaying expensive LLM requests on restart
actionable: When implementing agent state machines, split logic: synchronous reducers derive state, after_append hooks execute side effects—allows efficient replay on recovery
source_title: Make your own event-sourced agent harness using stream processors

claim: Agent system should be publicly routable (HTTP-accessible) and distributed across processes/languages so plugins from different authors compose without centralized server
actionable: Expose agent harness via HTTP so external plugins (Rust, TypeScript, etc.) can hook into the same event stream without central deployment
source_title: Make your own event-sourced agent harness using stream processors

claim: Circuit breaker pattern: rate-limit streams if >100 events/second to prevent infinite loops from agents triggering each other, pausing stream until manually resumed
actionable: Implement automated circuit breakers in multi-agent systems that pause event streams if event rate exceeds ~100/sec to prevent runaway feedback loops
source_title: Make your own event-sourced agent harness using stream processors

claim: Dynamic worker: deploy processors by appending JavaScript/TypeScript source code as an event, making any stream an agent without server deployment
actionable: Use dynamic worker pattern: append processor source code as event to stream, enabling agent-like behavior without separate service deployment
source_title: Make your own event-sourced agent harness using stream processors

claim: Stream processors (reducers) from multiple authors can inject context asynchronously: safety checker has ~200ms window before LLM request without blocking if it misses
actionable: Allow safety checkers to subscribe to agent events and inject guardrails within a bounded time window (e.g., 200ms) before LLM calls, gracefully degrading if checker is slow
source_title: Make your own event-sourced agent harness using stream processors

claim: Stream processor abstraction: state is derived from events via synchronous reducer, after_append hook enacts side effects—sufficient for building state-of-the-art coding agents
actionable: Model agent logic as: state = reduce(events) + side_effects(state_changes); avoid mixing synchronous derivation with async effects
source_title: Make your own event-sourced agent harness using stream processors

claim: Avoid before_hooks; use 200ms eventual consistency windows instead to allow third-party processors to contribute context without blocking agent performance
actionable: Instead of synchronous before-hooks, give async processors bounded time windows to inject context (200ms+) and proceed with default behavior if timeout
source_title: Make your own event-sourced agent harness using stream processors

claim: Sub-agents can be implemented as child paths in the stream hierarchy, with parent agents subscribing to result events to await async completion
actionable: Compose multi-agent systems by having agents append to child paths (e.g., /parent/sub-agent) and subscribe to result events for orchestration
source_title: Make your own event-sourced agent harness using stream processors

claim: Streaming chunks and tool calls should be modeled as events, not special cases, enabling uniform reduction logic and better composability across processors
actionable: Treat all agent state changes (LLM chunks, tool calls, errors) as first-class events in your log rather than side-channel data
source_title: Make your own event-sourced agent harness using stream processors

claim: UI for event sourced agents is also a stream processor that projects events to feed items, allowing UIs to be shared and reused across systems
actionable: Build agent UIs as stream processors that reduce events into renderable feed items, enabling UI reuse and composability
source_title: Make your own event-sourced agent harness using stream processors

claim: Rate limiting alone isn't sufficient for agent system safety; need authentication/authorization tied to event provenance to prevent unauthorized API use
actionable: Model permissions and authentication as events (make-stream-public) rather than external checks; track client provenance on each event for auditability
source_title: Make your own event-sourced agent harness using stream processors

claim: RAG pipelines with distributed processors can inject context asynchronously: index processor attempts to add context within time window, system proceeds if timeout
actionable: Design RAG agents where retrieval happens asynchronously: allow indexing processors bounded time to inject context before proceeding to LLM call
source_title: Make your own event-sourced agent harness using stream processors
